evals.prompt: I have a local coding agent that keeps overexplaining and waits too long before taking the first concrete action. Write me a better system prompt that makes it act faster, stay concise, and still surface risks when they matter.
evals.expected_output: The skill should produce a reusable agent prompt with explicit action bias, concise update behavior, escalation rules, and a small test set.
expectations: The output includes one paste-ready system prompt.
The prompt contains explicit guidance about fast first action or time-to-first-action.
The prompt includes a rule for concise updates or constrained verbosity.
The output also includes realistic test prompts.

evals.prompt: Turn this rough workflow into a reusable skill prompt: when someone asks for demand research, search real user feedback across Reddit, GitHub, and X, summarize repeated pain points, separate evidence from inference, and end with 3 product opportunities.
evals.expected_output: The skill should convert the workflow into a reusable skill or system prompt with triggers, workflow steps, evidence requirements, and output structure.
expectations: The output identifies that this is a reusable prompt rather than a one-off task prompt.
The final prompt includes trigger conditions or usage context.
The prompt separates sourced findings from inferred opportunities.
The prompt defines a concrete output structure.

evals.prompt: Rewrite this Claude prompt so it is easier to follow: 'Analyze this repo deeply, maybe use tools, probably search the web if needed, and tell me what to do.' The new version should use Claude-friendly structure and reduce ambiguity.
evals.expected_output: The skill should produce a Claude-oriented prompt with clearer instructions, tagged structure if helpful, explicit tool expectations, and clearer output requirements.
expectations: The output includes one rewritten prompt tailored for Claude.
The rewritten prompt is more explicit about tools, scope, and deliverable.
The output uses structured sections or XML-style tags.
The output includes a short explanation of the main changes.
